title: The Unfair Advantage
author: Ash Ali & Hasan Kubba
subline: What you don't know about success might be the only thing that matters.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <g transform="translate(800 1600)">
    <rect x="-280" y="-280" width="560" height="560" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="3"/>
    <text x="0" y="40" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="280" font-weight="300" fill="#ffffff" letter-spacing="-0.06em">M</text>
    <g transform="translate(-560 -560)">
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="200" height="200" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5"/>
      <text x="100" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="100" font-weight="300" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="-0.05em">P</text>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(360 -560)">
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="200" height="200" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5"/>
      <text x="100" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="100" font-weight="300" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="-0.05em">E</text>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(-560 360)">
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="200" height="200" fill="none" stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5"/>
      <text x="100" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="100" font-weight="300" fill="#cccccc" letter-spacing="-0.05em">T</text>
    </g>
    <g transform="translate(360 360)">
      <rect x="0" y="0" width="200" height="200" fill="none" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="2.5"/>
      <text x="100" y="140" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="100" font-weight="300" fill="#fc1c46" letter-spacing="-0.05em">K</text>
    </g>
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.6">
      <line x1="-360" y1="-360" x2="-280" y2="-280"/>
      <line x1="360" y1="-360" x2="280" y2="-280"/>
      <line x1="-360" y1="360" x2="-280" y2="280"/>
    </g>
    <line x1="360" y1="360" x2="280" y2="280" stroke="#fc1c46" stroke-width="1.5"/>
    <g font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" text-anchor="middle" fill="#4c4c4c">
      <text x="-460" y="-580">P · PASSION</text>
      <text x="460" y="-580">E · EXPERTISE</text>
      <text x="-460" y="600">T · TIMING</text>
    </g>
    <text x="460" y="600" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="13" font-weight="500" letter-spacing="0.18em" fill="#fc1c46">K · KNOWLEDGE</text>
  </g>
</svg>
rationale: The book's core equation M = P + E + T + K rendered as a 2x2 grid. The big M (success) is the white 560px outlined square; the four inputs are smaller squares at the corners. K = Knowledge is RED — the variable the author argues is the unfair advantage. The composition is a visual math problem with a hidden answer.
